common_sites: high organic matter or hydrophobic areas

symptoms.pattern: Irregular dry patches that do not respond evenly to normal irrigation
symptoms.plant: Wilt, blue-gray color, thinning, or dry firm surface
symptoms.soil: Water beading or poor infiltration can occur

checks: Compare moisture inside and outside the patch

management.immediate: Hand water hot spots and confirm moisture with a probe
management.program: Use wetting agents where hydrophobicity is confirmed; adjust irrigation distribution
management.cultural: Vent or needle tine if surface sealing or layering contributes

symptoms.pattern: Widespread or stress-zone decline during hot weather
symptoms.plant: Wilt, blue-gray color, thinning, reduced growth, or canopy collapse under severe stress

checks: Review recent high and night temperatures

management.immediate: Syringe or cool canopy only where appropriate; avoid overwatering saturated soils
management.program: Raise mowing height or reduce mowing stress during extreme heat
management.cultural: Improve air movement and reduce traffic when possible

symptoms.pattern: Footprinting, wilt, blue-gray color, then straw-brown dormancy or decline
symptoms.soil: Low soil moisture confirmed with probe or sensor

management.immediate: Rehydrate dry areas slowly enough to avoid runoff
management.cultural: Relieve compaction and encourage deeper rooting

symptoms.pattern: Soft turf, algae, thinning, shallow rooting, or increased disease pressure
symptoms.soil: Wet rootzone, poor oxygen, black layer risk in severe cases

checks: Look for algae or black layer indicators

management.immediate: Reduce irrigation where soil is saturated
management.cultural: Improve drainage, venting, and air movement

symptoms.pattern: Thinning in traffic patterns, poor infiltration, shallow rooting, weed invasion
symptoms.soil: Hard rootzone or restricted root growth

management.program: Use core aeration, solid tining, or other cultivation matched to site and season
management.cultural: Topdress and rebuild turf density after compaction relief

symptoms.pattern: Thin, weak turf with poor recovery and prolonged leaf wetness
symptoms.plant: Elongated growth, lower density, higher disease susceptibility

management.immediate: Reduce traffic and avoid overwatering shaded areas
management.program: Prune for light/air movement where feasible; choose shade-tolerant species
management.cultural: Raise mowing height and reduce nitrogen surges

symptoms.pattern: Linear or concentrated thinning following use patterns
symptoms.plant: Bruising, canopy thinning, compaction, poor recovery

management.program: Use cultivation, topdressing, overseeding/sodding, and traffic control
management.cultural: Match species/cultivar and mowing height to wear expectations

symptoms.pattern: Tan or brown injury following mower direction or high spots

checks: Look for pattern matching mowing passes

management.immediate: Raise height or adjust mower setup if injury is active
management.program: Smooth surfaces with topdressing and avoid abrupt height reductions
management.cultural: Allow recovery with appropriate fertility and irrigation

symptoms.pattern: Spray-pattern injury, overlaps, skips, or drift gradients
symptoms.plant: Bleaching, chlorosis, stunting, burn, or distorted growth depending on herbicide mode

checks: Check weather and turf species tolerance

management.immediate: Do not reapply until the cause is confirmed
management.program: Verify turf tolerance, rate, adjuvant, and weather restrictions before future applications
management.cultural: Support recovery with proper moisture and reduced stress

symptoms.pattern: Streaks, overlaps, edge patterns, or spill-shaped injury
symptoms.plant: Leaf burn or rapid browning after fertilizer application

management.immediate: Irrigate to move soluble salts if appropriate and not already saturated
management.program: Calibrate spreader and avoid applications during high-risk heat/drought periods
management.cultural: Repair severely injured areas after salts dissipate

symptoms.pattern: Bronzing, growth suppression, slower recovery, or uneven regulation
symptoms.plant: Shortened internodes and reduced clipping yield can be expected; stress occurs when suppression exceeds recovery capacity

management.immediate: Avoid stacking stress with mowing, drought, or heat
management.program: Adjust rate/interval to growth potential and species composition
management.cultural: Support recovery with moisture, nutrition, and reduced traffic stress

symptoms.soil: Black, foul-smelling anaerobic layer may be visible in the profile

management.immediate: Reduce saturation and improve oxygen movement
management.program: Vent, improve drainage, manage organic matter, and correct irrigation practices
management.cultural: Avoid practices that keep the rootzone chronically saturated
